review_title: Planview - A tool for resource database and financial management
description: Overall, its a solid tool to take good load of enterprise level needs.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: > Enterprise tool > Great for looking at various financial views at different levels from enterprise to program to project > Time-sheet tool for time tracking by resources > Help with milestones tracking
cons: Not so good to have schedule and cost tracked as package. Also, not sure - how suitable this is for SAFe methodologies.

description: Plainview Portfolios is a better way to manage your business and take profitability given the analytics and reporting aspect.
author: Rachid K.
pros: The prioritization is the most feature on this program cause it helps to prioritize tasks, save time and risks.
cons: The analytics and reporting is a helpful tool exist on this program to make new strategic goals to improve the business's profitability.

description: Planview is a good platform for manage project but need some evolution
author: Stefano C.
pros: The user experience on the platform, very easy to use
cons: The possibility to customize the platform

description: I love how the site is setup and easy to use and understand everything
author: Judy H.
pros: Easy to setup and easy to use very much understandable to use
cons: There are a few things that was not helpful in what a person in need
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Easy to use Project management portal
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease of use and detailed visibility to new projects and initiatives with timelines and a way to track revenue and growth.
cons: Although the portal is easy to use, the user interface may be a little difficult to navigate for beginners.

description: It is an average software that does not make it very user friendly. It has many resources within, but the cons outweigh the pros often. I would not be disappointed if we changed out software.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I would compare it to a microsoft project software. It is easy to manage multiple things at the same time and has many useful reports to run.
cons: It has a big learning curve and it difficult to learn on the fly. It does crash and glitch frequently and there is no way to undo a mistake with a clock of a button

description: Focus needs to be on ease of use. User adoption is not good
author: Shashank T.
pros: Has lots of features including capacity planning
cons: Capacity can be updated by individual users but it is not easy to use which creates lot of resistance from users to regularly update it.

review_title: Planview Project Portfolio not so user friendly
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Better than some other management systems but just as cumbersome as most. If you are using this then there is a business need to track project spends and allocation so useful for that.
cons: The interface is not super friendly and with an enterprise of hundreds of projects finding allocated projects and line items to show expenditures and add time tracking to them is a pain. Setting up recurring weekly timesheets also not very persistent for users,

description: It has different user roles which are similar to real life and has similar access. Application UI is good and easy to use
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Planview Enterprise One is a project management tool good for managing work, time and resources.
cons: It could be bit different to setup initial.

review_title: Good time tracking software for companies with many projects.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Each week it presents you with your most commonly added project/tasks that you can auto add to your time sheet for the week.
cons: The navigation/search functionality often doesn't work well when your company has a very large amount of projects.

review_title: Planview Portfolios is the Real PPM Program
author: Nsogbu W.
pros: I like that it is way too easy to manage projects and tasks without involving a single manual workflow.
cons: I do not have any issue that I encounter when using this program.
author_profession: Assistant Hatchery and Early Stage Manager

description: Potente en la gestión de proyectos y en planeación de los mismos
author: Cristian Henry G.
pros: Si bien su base es la gestión de proyectos tradicionales, el software contiene un potente espacio de gestión para proyectos ágiles permitiendo la colaboración y la elaboración de Kanban y seguimiento a actividades
cons: Se debe mejorar en los reportes estándar para brindar una mayor información histórica

review_title: My overall experience with Planview Portfolios.
author: Asnath G.
pros: It is simple to plan and optimize portfolios with this tool.It is simple to execute projects and portfolios strategies with Planview Portfolios.
cons: I haven't experienced any shortcomings with Planview Portfolios.

description: We switched to Planview from another software and it’s only been a few months, but honestly I wish we stayed with our previous software. Planview takes so much more time to work in and get the information we need.
author: Kelly C.
pros: When Planview finances are working properly I really enjoy the options for financial analysis across different projects. It allows for quick and easy comparison.
cons: What I like least about Planview is that the names of some of the columns don’t make sense. For example “Project Start Date” stops displaying the correct project date once the project starts.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Huge Tool / Lots of Admin needed
description: The Planview Sales and Support teams are great. They are very helpful. But unless you speak "Planview" you have no idea what they are talking about.
author: Richard H.
pros: It can perform any PPM and Resource Management function that any of the other tools do. The overall portfolio management can slice and dice the data very well.
cons: It is the most "UN-Intuitive" tool I've ever used. Without extensive training and administration support, it is next to impossible to use and train a casual end user or semi-casual PM.

description: I have been using Planview for more than 3 years now, and I liked it's simplicity in managing medium size/complex project. The best thing I liked is about the 'Export/Import capability of an MSP project at the task level'.
author: YERRISWAMY S.
pros: 1. Assigning resources to the individual and against the CAPEX and OPEX budget codes. 2. Reporting capabilities i.e., Status of the project, Estimated Hours vs Actual Hours, R/G/B status, Notes 3. Portfolio level management, and adding weightage to each and every project. 4. Easy to navigate and classic UI view. 5. Export/Import capability of an MSP project at the task level.
cons: Nothing to report as such, and pretty much everything seems to be fine.

review_title: Difficult to Implement but a Powerful Tool
description: Planview requires a lot of front end time investment to get the software up and running and for people to know how to use it. However once they do, it is a great tool to use especially with multiple sites to manage.
author: Allie S.
pros: Planview is very similar to Microsoft project which made implementation easier. It is also helpful for keeping track of financials across a variety of sites.
cons: Planview can be quite glitchy which causes efficiency to go down, and info can frequently be reset.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Planview- and effective way to manage project resources
description: I enjoy using planview and it helps our business move forward efficiently.
author: Amanda K.
pros: Planview has been easy to teach to people working on all levels of our projects. It’s fairly intuitive and user friendly.
cons: The auto generated orders can sometimes cause issues and extra time to correct. Starting from scratch can also be a bit difficult.

review_title: My organization uses Planview for time tracking and project assignment
author: William A.
pros: Relatively easy to use interface with the basic customization and access options you'd expect with this type of application.
cons: Very basic user interface with an outdated feel to the menus and dropdowns which detracts from the purpose of the application and it's feature set.
author_industry: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
